analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 4 finding(s) from 7 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Debug bus can reach crypto peripherals. reglk_wrapper clears all locks when jtag_unlock=1. The security of this depends entirely on the strength of jtag_unlock authentication (OBL-JTAG-UNLOCK-AUTH). Even without jtag_unlock, default unlocked state after reset allows debug to read/write secrets before software locks. This constitutes a candidate violation.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / Crypto peripheral registers (aes, hmac, sha256) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Debug master (via dm_master) Operation: read/write Object: Crypto peripheral registers (aes, hmac, sha256) Expected guard: jtag_unlock and/or peripheral lock controls (reglk_ctrl, acct_ctrl) Observed behavior: dm_master is connected to NOC via noc_axilite_bridge, allowing debug transactions to reach peripheral address ranges. No dedicated address filtering between debug bus and crypto peripherals. The reglk_wrapper uses jtag_unlock in its reset condition, clearing all lock bits when jtag_unlock is high, effectively disabling lock protection. Default lock state is all zeros (unlocked). Path: debug_req → dm_top → dm_master (AXI) → noc_axilite_bridge → NOC → peripheral noc_axilite_bridge → wrapper (e.g., aes0_wrapper) with acct_ctrl_i based on priv_lvl_i and acc_ctrl. If priv_lvl_i allows access, lock is the only barrier, and jtag_unlock can clear locks. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An attacker with JTAG access could assert jtag_unlock (if weak) and read/write all key registers. Even without jtag_unlock, debug transactions after reset can access peripherals before software locks them.

findings.summary: Reset default of reglk_ctrl is all zeros (unlocked). This defeats the purpose of the lock mechanism as an attacker can access all crypto registers before the lock is programmed. This is a security violation.
findings.vulnerability_category: clear/set initial state / reglk_ctrl output (lock bits) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: reglk_wrapper reset circuitry Operation: clear/set initial state Object: reglk_ctrl output (lock bits) Expected guard: All reglk_ctrl bits should reset to locked state (normally 1). Observed behavior: reglk_wrapper resets its reglk_mem array to 0 under the condition ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9). After a normal power-on reset (rst_ni de-asserted, jtag_unlock=0, rst_9=0), reglk_mem is cleared to 0. The reglk_ctrl_o output, which is a concatenation of reglk_mem, becomes all zeros. In the wrapper logic, a lock bit value of 0 corresponds to unlocked (allows reads/writes). Therefore, the default state is unlocked. Path: Global reset (rst_ni) → reglk_wrapper always block clears reglk_mem to 0 on posedge clk when condition holds. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: All peripherals are write/read enabled immediately after reset, allowing an attacker to write keys or read secrets before software configures locks.

findings.summary: The DMA interface ports (buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i, etc.) are present in riscv_peripherals, but the tool observations did not capture the DMA bridge or wrapper instantiation that would show routing to peripheral wrappers. The snippets only show debug, acct, reglk, and individual peripheral routings. The DMA path could not be traced.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / Crypto peripheral memory-mapped registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMA engine bus interface Operation: read/write Object: Crypto peripheral memory-mapped registers Expected guard: Same access-control mediation (acct_ctrl/reglk_ctrl) as CPU-initiated transactions or restricted address ranges. Observed behavior: The DMA interface ports (buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i, etc.) are present in riscv_peripherals, but the tool observations did not capture the DMA bridge or wrapper instantiation that would show routing to peripheral wrappers. The snippets only show debug, acct, reglk, and individual peripheral routings. The DMA path could not be traced. Path: DMA NOC interface → noc_axilite_bridge → peripheral AXI (inferred but not observed). Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If DMA can bypass locks, an attacker capable of programming DMA could read/write key registers.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need to inspect the DMA wrapper/bridge instantiation in riscv_peripherals.sv (around line 1600-1700) to confirm if DMA accesses go through acct/reglk wrappers. The provided tool observations did not include that region.

findings.summary: The design places extreme trust in jtag_unlock. If its generation is weak, an attacker can unlock all peripherals. This obligation remains inconclusive until dmi_jtag logic is reviewed.
findings.vulnerability_category: set jtag_unlock / jtag_unlock signal used by reglk_wrapper / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: jtag_unlock generation logic Operation: set jtag_unlock Object: jtag_unlock signal used by reglk_wrapper Expected guard: jtag_unlock asserted only after strong authentication (e.g., correct jtag_hash). Observed behavior: jtag_unlock is an output of dmi_jtag module, driven by jtag_hash, okey_hash, ikey_hash from fuse_mem, and testmode_i, we_flag_4. No RTL for dmi_jtag or fuse_mem provided. The connection in riscv_peripherals shows: .jtag_unlock_o (jtag_unlock) from i_dmi_jtag. The reglk_wrapper uses jtag_unlock to override lock reset (clear reglk_mem when high). There is no visible hardcoding of jtag_unlock in riscv_peripherals.sv. Path: dmi_jtag (line 275-297) → jtag_unlock → reglk_wrapper; dmi_jtag inputs include jtag_hash, okey_hash, ikey_hash from fuse_mem. Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If jtag_unlock can be set without proper authentication (e.g., testmode_i override, hardcoded constant, or weak hash implemented in dmi_jtag), an attacker could permanently disable all locks and access all secrets via JTAG. The design is high-risk because jtag_unlock provides a single point of override.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The RTL of dmi_jtag and fuse_mem modules is not available in the provided observations. Without it, the authentication strength cannot be assessed. Also, the effect of testmode_i and we_flag_4 on jtag_unlock inside dmi_jtag is unknown.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
